intro: This page serves as the operational guide for the panel. We highly recommend reading it thoroughly before you begin.
quickTips: When creating a new user, set their status to 'On Hold' so the expiration timer only starts upon their first connection.
Never leave the note field empty; add a brief description like 'monthly', 'test', or 'VIP' to make account management much easier later.
If a customer reports a broken link, simply click the copy or QR icon from the users table to generate a fresh, valid link.
Before deleting an account, always revoke its subscription first to ensure old links are permanently deactivated.
Pro tip: The Users search box is highly versatile—it accepts subscription links, tokens, keys, UUIDs, and full config links.

sections.title: Search users by link or ID
sections.description: You can find a customer's account instantly by entering almost any piece of their connection data.
steps: Paste a subscription link (token, key, or username/key) into the Users search box.
Enter a config link (vless/vmess/trojan/shadowsocks) to immediately identify its owner.
Searching by UUID, credential key, or raw token is also fully supported.
hints: For rapid troubleshooting, paste the customer's exact config link into the search box.
The search engine accepts both raw values and full URLs.

sections.title: Get a single config
sections.description: If you need to provide a standalone config to a customer, you can easily grab it from the Links tab.
steps: On the Users page, click the edit icon for the desired user.
Navigate to the "Links" tab.
Use the QR or copy button right there to grab the single config.
hints: QR codes are fastest for sharing to mobile devices; copying the link is better for text messaging.
If the main subscription link is experiencing issues, a single config serves as a great fallback.

sections.title: Create user
sections.description: Create a new user and hand over the link instantly—no server-side configuration required.
steps: On the Users page, click “Create user” to open the dialog.
Fill username, status (active or on hold), expiry date, and data cap.
Choose the right service so the correct link is generated for the customer.
Set IP limit and add a note if needed.
Enable Auto Renew if you want hands-off renewals.
Save and share the link or QR with the user.
hints: Use the 'On Hold' status to keep an account ready without starting the clock until the customer connects.
Always add a brief note so you remember the specific purpose of the account.
Setting the IP limit to 1 or 2 is an effective way to prevent unauthorized sharing.

sections.title: Service auto-assign via Service Inbound
sections.description: Utilize Service Inbound tags to automatically assign a service when creating or editing a user.
steps: Go to the Services page, open a service, and create its "Service inbound". The tag format is setservice-<service_id>.
In the Create/Edit user form, if no service is selected, choose only that Service Inbound tag in the Inbounds section.
The panel will automatically assign the linked service and apply its specific settings.
Note: If you manually select a service, any inbound selections are ignored and the service's default settings take over.
hints: The Service Inbound must be selected by itself. Do not combine it with other inbound tags.
If you require manual inbound control, avoid using the setservice-* tags entirely.
For troubleshooting, refer to the Tutorial button on the Node or Service page.

sections.title: Renew user
sections.description: You don’t need to create a brand new account to extend time or add data limits.
steps: In the users table, click the username or the edit icon to open the settings drawer.
Select a new expiry date or increase the data limit allowance.
If the user was previously disabled or limited, change their status back to "Active".
Save your changes. The customer's existing link will continue to work seamlessly.
hints: Enable Auto Renew in this same window if you want the account to automatically top-up upon reaching its limits.
If you need to restart their usage tracking from zero, simply use the Reset Usage button.

sections.title: On-hold user
sections.description: Use this status when you want to prepare an account in advance but delay activation until the first connection.
steps: Set the status to "On Hold" in the create/edit form.
Set the "On hold duration" to 2-5 days so the account expires automatically if they never connect.
For added security, set the IP limit to 1 or 2.
Share the subscription link. After the first successful connection, the status flips to Active and tracking begins.
hints: If the on-hold period expires before the customer connects, you can either extend the duration or delete the account.
Once the account becomes active, you can easily extend data or time from the exact same window.

sections.title: My account
sections.description: See your own usage and update your password from one place.
steps: Open "My account" from the sidebar.
Top cards show your used/remaining/total data and user limits.
Use the Daily usage chart with a date filter to track your usage over time.
In Change password, enter current + new password or use the random generator button.
hints: Narrow the date range for charts if you want faster loading.

sections.title: Nodes
sections.description: This section covers three main areas: general node overview, setup instructions, and managing node Secrets.

modalExample.title: Node certificate created
modalExample.copyHint: Copy this certificate and place it on the node server.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.description: Displays the operational status, service/API ports, usage coefficient, and total traffic for each node.

subsections.title: How to set up
steps: Click 'Add node' and fill in the name, domain/IP, service port, API port, usage coefficient, and an optional data cap.
If you require standalone configurations per node, fill in xray_config, sing_config, or hysteria_config; otherwise, leave them blank to inherit global settings.
If you utilize Nobetci, enable the option and define the monitor port.
After saving, a modal will display the unique node Secret; copy or download it immediately (each node requires its own).
Example: For teh1.example.com with service port 62050 and API 62051, place the generated Secret securely on that specific server.
Once registered, verify connectivity by checking the Hosts/Integrations section.
hints: The Secret is generated after saving and can be retrieved later from the node page if needed.
Ensure your service and API ports are open, and that your server time is synchronized via NTP.
Leverage the usage coefficient for manual load balancing (e.g., assigning a '2' to a higher-capacity node).
Never share a Secret across multiple nodes; generate a unique one for every server.
If a node shows as offline, double-check your firewall, DNS, and Hosts configurations.

sections.title: Admins
sections.description: Covers the admins page overview, comprehensive management guide, and detailed role explanations.

subsections.title: Overview
subsections.description: The table displays the assigned role, status, data/user limits, and general activity stats for every admin.

subsections.title: How to manage
steps: Click 'Add admin', set a username and password, and select a role: Full Access, Sudo, Reseller, or Standard.
Define a data_limit and users_limit to set the boundaries of their operational scope.
Use the Enable/Disable toggle to temporarily pause or resume access for an admin and their associated sub-users.
Deleting an admin performs a soft delete, which safely disables their sub-users as well.
hints: If you've increased limits or reset usage for a paused admin, remember to re-enable their account.
Reserve the Full Access role for primary owners; use Sudo or Standard roles for your support teams.

subsections.title: Full Access
subsections.description: The highest tier of access, offering absolute control over all sections and sensitive settings.
steps: Create and edit roles, services, nodes, and integrations without any restrictions.
Define data caps and access rights for all other admins and users.
Assign this role exclusively to system owners and keep the total count to an absolute minimum.
hints: Carefully review permissions before elevating an account to this role.
Mandate strong passwords and Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) for Full Access accounts.

subsections.title: Sudo
subsections.description: Provides high operational access without system ownership rights; ideal for senior technical and support staff.
steps: Create and edit users, services, and nodes (excluding core ownership configurations).
Capable of handling most daily operations but restricted from ownership-level modifications.
Keep the number of Sudo admins small to minimize security risks.
hints: If a staff member doesn't need to manage other Full Access accounts, the Sudo role is sufficient.
Periodically review their caps and activity logs for auditing purposes.

subsections.title: Reseller
subsections.description: Designed for sales agents with defined quotas; allows management strictly for their own sub-users.
steps: Create and manage sub-users within the confines of their assigned users_limit and data_limit.
No access granted to sensitive system architecture or sections outside of client management.
The perfect fit for business partners and sales affiliates.
hints: Users in the 'On Hold' status do not consume the users_limit quota; only active ones do.
Set these limits to reflect your agreed partnership contract.

subsections.title: Standard
subsections.description: The most restricted access level; operators can only view or edit sections explicitly enabled for them.
steps: Excellent for trainees, junior support, or restricted operators.
Access to each section (Users, Admins, Services, etc.) must be toggled on individually.
Self Permissions dictate which specific modules are visible on their 'My account' page.
hints: For a read-only view, turn on can_view and ensure can_edit remains off.
Grant permissions gradually as the operator requires more access.

dialogSections.title: Basic info & status
dialogSections.description: The top section handles identity, operational status, time, and data quotas.

fields.name: Username
fields.detail: Must be 3–32 characters with no spaces. Keep it simple and readable.
tips: Append a 'trial' or 'test' suffix to temporary accounts so they stand out in the dashboard.

fields.name: Status
fields.detail: Active for normal use, On Hold to delay tracking until the first connect, Limited/Expired/Disabled to suspend access.
tips: The On Hold status pauses expiry and data tracking until the first successful handshake.

fields.name: Expire
fields.detail: The cutoff date for access. Leave this blank for an unlimited lifespan.
tips: Quick-add buttons (e.g., +30 days) are conveniently located above the table.

fields.name: Data limit (GB)
fields.detail: Total data quota in gigabytes. Leave blank for unlimited bandwidth.
tips: For trial accounts, 10–50 GB is usually more than enough.

fields.name: IP limit
fields.detail: Maximum number of simultaneous IPs allowed. Keep it at 1 or 2 to heavily discourage link sharing.

dialogSections.title: Service
dialogSections.description: Pick which service the account should use.

fields.name: Service
fields.detail: Select the right service so the settings needed to build the link are applied automatically.

dialogSections.title: Subscription link
dialogSections.description: How to get the customer's connection link.

fields.name: Subscription link / QR
fields.detail: After hitting save, grab the freshly generated link or QR code directly from the users table.

dialogSections.title: Auto Renew
dialogSections.description: Configure rules to keep accounts active seamlessly without manual intervention.

fields.name: Data limit
fields.detail: The data quota to apply automatically when the cap is hit.
tips: Toggle 'Add remaining' to roll over any unused data traffic into the next billing cycle.

fields.name: Time limit (days)
fields.detail: The number of days added to the expiry date. Zero indicates no time cap.

fields.name: Fire on either
fields.detail: When enabled, the renewal triggers as soon as either the time or quota limit is reached.

dialogSections.title: Notes & contact info
dialogSections.description: Supplementary details for better account management and customer support.

fields.name: Note
fields.detail: A brief description of the account or deal. Highly useful for future searches.

fields.name: Telegram ID / Contact number
fields.detail: Customer contact information for troubleshooting and follow-ups.

statuses.title: On Hold (waiting for first connection)
statuses.description: The account is created but hasn't established a connection yet. The first successful handshake switches it to Active and starts the timer.
actions: Ideal for trial periods or onboarding brand new customers.
If the on-hold duration runs out without a connection, extend the timer or remove the account.
Keep the IP limit low to prevent link abuse before activation.

statuses.title: Active (in use)
statuses.description: The standard operational state. Data usage, expiry times, and connection logs are actively tracked.
actions: Top up data, extend the expiry date, or reset usage metrics at any time.
If you need to pause access without deleting the account, switch the status to Disabled.

statuses.title: Limited (out of data)
statuses.description: The allocated data cap has been reached and the connection is currently restricted.
actions: Add more data, reset the usage, or switch the status back to Active after receiving payment.
Enable Auto Renew with the "Add remaining" option to prevent unused traffic from being lost.

statuses.title: Expired (date passed)
statuses.description: The expiration date has passed, denying the user further access.
actions: Push the expiry date forward or reset usage, then reactivate the account.
If the account has been dormant for an extended period, revoke the subscription and issue a brand new link.

faqs.question: When is the best time to use the 'On Hold' status?
faqs.answer: Whenever you want to provision an account but delay the start of its billing cycle until the customer actually connects.
bullets: Trial accounts where the user might not connect immediately.
Customers who require the link beforehand but will pay/activate later.
Any scenario where you want to send links early but start the clock later.

faqs.question: What is the fastest way to renew a user?
faqs.answer: Simply open the user dialog, update the expiry date or data quota, and save.
bullets: For a hands-off approach, turn on Auto Renew inside the same window.
Use the 'Reset usage' button if you prefer to zero out their current data counters.

faqs.question: What’s the difference between Active, On Hold, and Disabled?
faqs.answer: Active means full operational access; On Hold pauses timers until the very first connection is made; Disabled forcefully suspends access until you manually restore it.

faqs.question: A customer is reporting connection issues—where do I start?
faqs.answer: Verify three critical points: the status is set to Active, the expiry/quota limits haven't been exceeded, and the link they are using is current.
bullets: Generate a fresh QR code or subscription link from the users table and send it to them.
If their status is Limited or Expired, extend their allowances accordingly.
If you suspect they shared their link, lower their IP limit and reset their usage.

faqs.question: What exactly is a subscription and how do I create one?
faqs.answer: A subscription encompasses the user account and its connection link. Head to the Users page and click 'Create user' to set one up.
bullets: Data limit = the maximum data volume (in GB).
Time limit = the expiration date; after this, the connection drops.
Service = a predefined template bundling specific inbounds, quotas, and lifespans.

faqs.question: Common terminologies in subscription settings
faqs.answer: Here are definitions of key terms you will encounter while creating or editing accounts:
bullets: Auto delete: Automatically removes the user entirely once their time or quota ends.
Auto renew: Automatically replenishes traffic and time limits.
Add remaining: Carries over any unused traffic into the next billing cycle.
Revoke access: Invalidates the old links (Revoke Subscription).
Reset usage: Zeroes out the current usage tracking counters.
Enable/disable: Temporarily grants or blocks connection access.
Connection method: via the subscription link or QR code imported into clients like V2Ray, Reality, or ShadowSocks.

samples.note: Trial account; link delivered but no connection established yet.
Monthly subscription; Auto Renew is currently enabled.
Data allowance reached; requires a top-up or usage reset.
Past the expiry date; account will reactivate upon extension.

panelIntro.title: Panel overview
panelIntro.description: Rebecca is a VPN service management panel. After evaluating several options, the Vento team found it covered everything they needed in structure and features, so they stopped building an in-house panel and adopted Rebecca as their main platform. This panel is used to create and manage customer accounts.
bullets: Create and renew users with active, on hold, limited, and expired statuses, plus links, QR codes, and Auto Renew.
Quickly search for users by username, link, or subscription code.
My Account: view your daily usage report and change your password.
